item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: An Afghan Refugee's Journey Through Food
item.description:
1. This is the story of my coming to Australia as a refugee from Afghanistan and my life in Australia, told through food.
2. The very first thing I ever ate in Australia was a Lamington. It’s a square of sponge cake coated in chocolate icing and rolled in coconut. I had been in a refugee camp for two years, living off rice, beans, and millet porridge. There had never been enough and there certainly had never been sweets or cakes. Even fruit had been a rare and luxurious treat. I had just gotten off the plane to Australia and while I was waiting someone offered me a Lamington. The memory of biting into is so clear in my mind, I remember everything, what the room looked like, where I was standing, what clothes I was wearing and most of all the taste. In that moment it was the best thing I had ever tasted, soft and sweet. I have since had them many times, but none can compare to that first one.
3. The next food I want to talk about is Vegemite. But first I need to talk about something else. The Toaster. Before coming to Australia, I had never seen a Toaster or sliced bread in a loaf. I was put in transitional housing, and it came with a toaster. I didn’t know what it was, so I left it alone. I asked my case worker and she showed me, I was amazed. I thought “Its like a little naan oven that doesn’t use fire”. When the toast jumped out with a bang, I screamed just a little.
4. First, I tried the toast with butter, which I loved, it was like ghee, and I love ghee. Then I decided to try Vegemite, it seemed like the quintessential Australia food, and I felt like I needed to try it. I buttered the toast and spread the Vegemite thinly over it, the Vegemite looked like shoe polish and it smelled like strong meat broth and chemicals despite neither being present. The first bite tasted bitter and salty and where it touched the exposed skin of my lips it burnt. I threw it away and washed my face like I had a chemical burn. In a few days I tried again, this time careful to not get it on my skin. I spread it even thinner and took a careful bite, my lips pulled back. This time I could see the appeal, it was rich, savoury, and unique. I finished the toast, but I do not have any desire to eat it again. I will stick with butter.
5. After a few months I was able to get work and move into my own place, a small, terraced cottage in a smallish mountain village, it has a little backyard with beautiful birds and flowers. My neighbour is a nice older lady who one day asked me if I would like to go with her to the seaside (about 90 minutes away). I had never seen the ocean up close before nor felt it on my skin. I can’t swim, but I let the waves wash against the bottom of legs and feet. It filled me with a joy that’s hard to explain.
6. For lunch we went to a seafood restaurant. I really wanted to try lobster and crab, but I had heard that sometimes they are placed into boiling water while still alive. For me that is haram, and I would not like that to happen to any living creature. So, I asked the waitress, she didn’t know, but said she would ask the chef. The chef came out, she was a blonde, white Australian in her 30’s. She assured me that she killed them first before cooking and said that I could come watch if I wanted to make sure. I told her that I trusted her, but she said she would like me to watch, so I agreed. It was an open kitchen so I could watch without actually entering. The chef talked the whole while, explaining everything, much of it I missed as my English could not keep up. I was so honoured that she cared that much about making sure it was right for me. The food itself when it was finished, was amazing. It had been fried in butter and tasted like heaven.
7. A big community event happened in my village. I went along and it seemed like everyone was there (Covid didn’t affect us until Omnicom came in the last month). In the middle of the event was a square shaped food stall run by a charity with barbeques set up. It was what Australians call the “Sausage Sizzle”. There was possibly the largest man I had ever seen running it, I later learned he was Samoan, he seemed like he was twice my height and 3x my weight (I admit that I am short and skinny, so that’s not hard), but he spoke in a soft gentle voice and had soft gentle eyes. He saw me looking and asked if I wanted to have some? I said that I didn’t think I could. He nodded and said he had vegetarian sausages that he cooked on a different barbeque, and could I eat those? I smiled and agreed. He grabbed a napkin, put a slice of bread on it and slapped a sausage diagonally across it and asked, “Do you want onion?” I said I didn’t know, and he laughed. It was a nice laugh. He is a nice man and I hope that life will always make him laugh. He said that he would give me one with and one without. He handed them to me and said, “Get some sauce on em”. I put mustard as I find tomato sauce too sweet. I tried to pay him, but he laughed and waved it away. He pulled off his glove and reached into his pocket and paid with his own money. I tried to protest but he just waved it away again and said it was his pleasure. I thanked him and he said it was “no worries love”. I bit into it, and it tasted so good. I walked through the crowd eating it and trying not to spill mustard on myself. People grinned at me, and I grinned back. I was happy. The war, death, and suffering that has been my whole life, felt like it had faded just a tiny bit. I wasn’t scanning the crowds looking for gunmen or potential bombs. My ears weren’t straining to hear the sound of approaching helicopters, drones or military vehicles. I wasn’t feeling guilty for being alive when so many weren’t. I realised that I finally felt like I belonged. Thank you for reading. If you want to know more of my story, check my profile. Special thanks to my best friend & fellow Afghan @HeyArabella.
item.dc:creator: AfghanAveen

item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: My name is Melanie and I'm self taught Artist from a small European country named Slovenia and here is another one painting I'm sharing for #artcrawl
item.description:
The name of this acrylic painting is "When the Summer ends" and I made it as a "goodbye" to the summer 2021.
I made it on a 35 x 40cm (13,7 x 15,7 inches) big canvas and finish it in one evening (so in only fev hours)
My inspiration for the painting was this place and this photo that I took on the last day of my vacation in a beautiful Slovenian historic harbor town named Piran.
Here is also a short video where you can see some more details of the painting where I put all of my heart, my emotions and love for that place into it... Yeah, I call it love.
Here you can see some other of my "Summer themed" paintings... I really hope that you like it as much as I do. :) And if you would choose to suport me and my art, I will be grateful and happy to the stars and beyond, because you'll make all of my dreams come true. You can support me here: https://www.etsy.com/shop/MelanieJArt Or visit my Insta: @melaniejakeljart Thank you! :)
item.dc:creator: MelanieJakeljArt

item.title: I lost 55kg (121lbs) this year!
item.description:
I had gastric sleeve surgery! After 6 back surgeries, i found it impossible to work out without being in pain. I was also on medication to help with my back that made me gain weight. Catch 22.
After the surgery, I was able to move around more with less pain and was able to join a soccer team! Icing on the cake was scoring a goal in the finals! Wooo
Starting bjj next week. Super excited. Don’t let your dreams be dreams. Weight loss surgery is fucking hard. Weight loss in anyway is fucking hard. Whatever works for you, do it!
item.dc:creator: Mooseyxoxo

item.title: Did you know imgur has mental health help built into the app?
item.description:
Go to your personal page
Click the widget thing upper right
Scroll down and select 'wellness'
Ta-da! Scroll for the suicide prevention hotline numbers in US and UK
Plus mental health tips
You can ask for help if you are concerned about a fellow imgurian
And internal resources of imgurians willing to talk. Please take care of yourself and reach out if you need help. You matter and it can get better.
item.dc:creator: theblankestblankintown

item.title: Phone dump from a newly diagnosed ADHD adult
item.description: Edit: was not anticipating most viral!! As ppl have said, some of these fall into other diagnoses or are accurate for a lot of humans! Absolutely true. Please talk to your doctor or therapist if you think you might need diagnosis. For me, this diagnosis changed my life and I suddenly realized a lot of my everyday “processes” were coping skills. My anxiety and depression are the best they have ever been, bc my anxiety and depression were manifestations of my ADHD. Aaaand because I made this after my adderall wore off, some of these are just goofy or resonated with me. Great accounts for ADHD content (and where I pulled these from): (ADHD) Memes & More, Therapy Memes Group, The ADHD Gift, Jenn has ADHD, ADHD Interrupted.
item.dc:creator: berniebug

item.title: Everyone's a gangster until its time to be a gangster

item.title: I made this, but had to give it away
item.description:
It's a book nook: a miniature streetscape hidden away in a bookshelf. Inspired by a Japanese maker I saw on imgur many years ago. It was a joy to make, bittersweet to finish and impossible to give away. Finished product first (rules are rules); story and build to follow.
Taking me about 6 months start to finish, it is a combination of carved wood and foam, a mirror, 3D printed parts, clay, glass, fabric, real dried flowers, LEDs, and a destroyed encyclopaedia.... Custom modelled, constructed and hand painted. I probably glued my fingers to every single component at one time or another.
Starting out the modelling/designing in old faithful SketchUp. So. It was intended as a gift for my long-term partner at the time...
she had a love of all things tiny - please hold your jokes until the end (Starting with EVA foam pieces, and building up layers of paint)
...she also had an infectious sense of wonder
And I loved to surprise her with my presents each year
So I kept this gift close to my chest. Above you can see me constructing tiny streetlights using plastic and wiring them up using Woodland Scenics excellent LED light range.
The first of the 3D printed parts being test fit. A bit warped but nothing a bit of heat couldn't fix! Making something like this in secret with limited privacy meant some serious off-peak construction times.
So many tiles. I still dream of them sometimes.
The paint jobs were starting to look good. Unfortunately, our relationship was not.
The cracks were starting to show. Building up the facade using modelling clay and scratching out the stonework lines using a trusty toothpick
We were drifting apart, and we both knew something was coming. We broke up. It tore my heart. But after a few months I realised I couldn't leave this unfinished.
So I kept at it. Most of the parts together! The book nook utilises an angled mirror to give the illusion of infinite depth when you look in at it. I designed the model to have slightly different appearance on the reflected side to make it look more convincing.
Committing sacrilege and cutting an encyclopaedia to bits. But wikipedia exists so...
Wiring up a hidden switch to toggle the electronics
Final wiring test before gluing forever. One light in the window, one overhead, and one in the sconce.
The encyclopaedia parts holding everything inside. The front face is detachable and held on with magnets.
Every little detail I added felt like I was taking steps towards closure. Here you can see little vases that are filled with real tiny flowers dried and painted. I designed the walls of the model to hinge so you could open it up and appreciate all these stupidly tiny details.
Even a minuscule newspaper to rest on the step. Somewhere along the way I realised that I couldn't hold onto it. It was always intended as a gift, and there could only be one recipient. It was to become the literal bookend of our time together.
And here it is in its final resting place. I organised a time to hand it over -- it's a good thing we stayed on great terms.
But against all odds I had maintained the surprise
And the reaction of joy and wonder was everything I could have hoped for
We weren't meant to be, but I'm so glad that I didn't give up on this and I was able to let it all go. The time spent together and on this was all worth it, and the results are treasured.
The end result opened wide
All the electronics and battery hidden neatly away.
Stretching out to infinity with the help of a mirror
Old life. New life. Thanks for listening to my tale! I hope you enjoyed sharing my creation as much as I enjoyed making it.
Cat tax. She's friendly I swear
item.dc:creator: MonforyHorrors

item.title: The legend.
item.dc:creator: Krashtestdummy

item.title: My Art - My Cosplay
item.description:
My Artcrawl Submission is my cosplay! A final example of my ary is above with me as Ada Wong from Resident Evil -- but to get this to that point is usually super extensive.
First up: I've had to really learn how a camera really works, because all of my work is shot by me - which is why you'll often see me holding a small remote whenever I'm not holding a camera. Shooting your own cosplay not only means learning the ins and outs of a camera over the years, but it also means:
Lighting! This is one of my many "Set" setups. I change them up often, but this is one of my go to backgrounds for initial cosplay tests, and you may notice that behind that curtain is the mirror you often see me take pics in front of for my Imgur posts! I find the mirror post to be the best image to upload here because it shows the full cosplay - but that is always like one picture out of the thousand I take.
I usually have about 4 cosplays in production at any one time. And all of them are either hand crafted by me, or purchased and augmented to fit better and improve the quality. I augment a lot, because if you know me, you might be aware than typically "femme" clothing doesn't fit exactly the way it should on me. I'll leave it at that. Anyway, initial creation is is a ton of work, but is also one of my favorite parts - gathering and augmenting to get it as close as possible to your character is a ton of fun! So, for example - last year I simultaneously had these 4 cosplays all occurring at once: Above is 2B...
I also had Ada Wong in the works...
Elizabeth from Bioshock...
And Classic Scarlet Witch...
While also crafting my Ciri wig!
All of this leads up to the shoot day, and a final product (Me as Mara Jade Above) A shoot day usually involves about 6 hours of prep work before I can even start taking pictures. A full day of shooting usually means I am up from 6am until about 3am (so nearly 24 hours). So by the end of the day I have gotten all the pictures taken and transferred (hours and hours of color correction happens later), and then I am finally able to eat proper food... Well, thanks for checking out my ArtCrawl! Imgur has always been super supportive of me and I love you guys!
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: People calling Peter Doocy stupid is a long and storied Washington pastime.
item.description: If you didn’t know, the stupid son of a bitch Peter Doocy is the stupid son of stupid son of a bitch Steve Doocy, one of the mannequins on Fox and Friends that holds up the ceiling in-between catheter commercials.

item.title: Going to hell if you laugh?

item.title: Thrift Store Tuesday: Red Valentine's Day Dress, Vintage Nightgown, and Awesome Plus Size Disney Dress
item.description:
Missing waist belt but for the price of $4 it was worth it.
No pockets though :(
Can't go out in it on a windy day, Lol.
This lovely vintage Miss Elaine brand Nightgown.
Just beautiful mint flowy condition. Probably 70s-80s?
Also foumd an awesome Disney dress, in plus size 3X. Looks new.
Love the newspaper print style! I like to thrift and resell what I find to help get by and help afford my meds and other expenses. On occasion I keep things for myself. I like sharing what I find. My chat privileges were banned, so I can only read comments. I cannot read or respond to messages. I hope everyone has a nice Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: thehipsgoddess

item.dc:creator: reformedxile

item.title: We need more people like Brian
item.description: In Dublin, Ireland, Brian, a retired man just living off a small pension, does something special every single night. He spends those nights making curry for homeless people. And every night, he makes 50 tubs of it. Brian is cooking volunteer for "A Lending Hand,” which is a non-profit organization that delivers meals and offers sleeping bags to those living on the streets in Dublin. Says Brian: “I’m delighted to be able to cook and having been trained by a master chef from India. It gives me great solace to be able to bring a hot meal to those hungry and homeless.” And you gotta believe that it’s people like Brian who make this big old world a better place to live in.
item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: Hot mic: Joe Biden just called Peter Doocy a stupid son of a bitch￼
item.description: I mean it’s an accurate observation, but maybe turn off the mic. And who’s the short dude who took off his mask to the holler his question?
